items.title: Shark danger from rotting whale forces ocean swim event to move

children.content: A 40-tonne sperm whale carcass has forced the relocation of Tasmania's biggest open water swim. Organisers of the event had hoped it would be moved, but the state's environment department says that was not feasible.

gallery.altText: a pink and grey whale carcass in white and aqua waves

thumbnail.altText: a pink and grey whale carcass in white and aqua waves

avatar.alt: A woman with straight, fair, shoulder-length hair smiles. She wears a black top.

items.title: 'People assume we're not real': MPs open up about mental health challenges

children.content: A national study has found 70 per cent of politicians feel their job is affecting their mental health. The ABC spoke to three Tasmanian MPs, including the state's premier, about their experiences.

image.altText: A composite image using headshots of Ella Haddad, Jeremy Rockliff and Ruth Forrest.

items.title: Study finds decades of 'intensive' logging worsens bushfire risk

children.content: Scientists have studied satellite images of a Tasmanian bushfire and found regrowth from extensive logging and recent bushfires has "absolutely" increased the risk of more severe bushfires.

image.altText: Forest lit up by unseen bushfire flames.

items.title: With most of it not fit to sell, this op shop fixes donated items to reduce landfill

children.content: Every year, lots of new textile items are imported to the Australian market … about 1.55 billion of them. Fast fashion is a leading contributor to overproduction and makes reuse difficult.

gallery.altText: A woman looking at clothes on a rack in an op shop

thumbnail.altText: A woman looking at clothes on a rack in an op shop

avatar.alt: A woman with long, brown hair in  pink jumper.

items.title: Call for 'collective action' to address gendered violence in Tasmania

children.content: A new platform, dubbed Justice For Her, is centred on making small, actionable pledges to help people overcome the overwhelming statistics on gendered violence, which often lead to inaction.

image.altText: Sally Haynes and Carolyn Self launching "Justice for Her" 2026-04-08 10:04:00

items.title: 'Made it very attractive': Free rides luring thousands more onto buses

children.content: The temporary ability to catch public transport for free is turning some Tasmanians into "converts", while others are using it for new experiences.

image.altText: A man in a 'PROTECT TAKAYNA' shirt stands in front of a bus going to Devonport.

items.title: Emperor penguins, Antarctic fur seals now endangered due to climate change

children.content: The iconic emperor penguin and the Antarctic fur seal are edging closer to extinction, according to a projection by the world's largest environmental network.

articles.title: The emperor penguin and Antarctic fur seal are now considered endangered

children.content: The iconic emperor penguin and the Antarctic fur seal are edging closer to extinction, according to a projection by the world's largest environmental network.

image.altText: Eleven black and white penguins slide along ice in a row.

gallery.altText: A penguin chicken stands in the foreground. Camera crews are in the distance.

items.title: Artemis II crew prepares to ride 'fireball' back to Earth

children.content: Here's a breakdown of how the Orion space capsule copes with high temperatures, slows down, and what happens as it splashes down.

gallery.altText: NASA Orion space capsule floats in the ocean with orange balloons and a navy ship in the background.
The Orion space capsule floats over the ocean, slowed down by balloons.

items.title: Australians less likely to get bypass surgery now than 20 years ago

children.content: John Ross may be Australia's longest surviving double bypass patient. Here's how has the cardiac operation has evolved over the past 50 years.

image.altText: A black and white photo of a surgical operation.

items.title: A look at the sea mines Iran claims are in the Strait of Hormuz

children.content: Iran claims to have littered a central shipping passage in the Strait of Hormuz with sea mines, in a move analysts say could be a "coercive threat" to maintain its grip on the crucial waterway.

image.altText: A smartphone screen shows traffic in Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: UK charity sues Prince Harry for defamation

children.content: Prince Harry co-founded Sentebale 20 years ago in honour of his late mother, Princess Diana, but now the organisation is suing him for defamation.

image.altText: Prince Harry smiles in front of Sentebale branding, wearing a khaki button-up shirt

items.title: British child sex offender faces possible visa review after avoiding deportation

children.content: A British child sex offender who avoided deportation is facing a possible visa review, after an ABC investigation.

image.altText: A swing at an Adelaide playground site.

items.title: 'Really big deal': P-pop group to make history at Coachella

children.content: As one of the world's largest music festivals gets underway this weekend, a pop group from the Philippines will make history.

gallery.altText: A group of girls on stage hold microphones and appear to be singing.

thumbnail.altText: A group of girls on stage hold microphones and appear to be singing.

items.title: Hull 'robbed' at national champs as rival disqualified after 1,500m clash

children.content: Jess Hull had been on track to claim a fourth-straight 1,500m national title before being clipped by Claudia Hollingsworth, bringing her reign crashing down.

image.altText: Runner Jess Hull falls and has arms splayed out in front of her

items.title: Alex de Minaur downed by local hero at Monte Carlo Masters

children.content: Alex de Minaur will slip from his career-high ranking after missing a chance to emulate his semifinal run at the Monte Carlo Masters, beaten in three sets by Monaco's Valentin Vacherot.

image.altText: Alex de Minaur completes his forehand swing over his shoulder during a tennis match.

items.title: 'Cosplay is not consent': The rules of dressing up for conventions

children.content: Cosplayers say their hobby has had an astronomical rise in popularity over the past decade. While the subculture promotes "no limits" creativity, there are still strict rules.

thumbnail.altText: A man and woman dressed in costume

items.title: ABC 50/50 quiz: A classic poem, oyster recipes and Itchy and Scratchy

children.content: Every question has only two options: one is right, and the other is wrong. You have an even chance of guessing correctly each time.

image.altText: A cartoon cat and mouse sit on rocking chairs on a porch. Lemonade is on a table between them

items.title: Debra Adelaide's best friend died by suicide. She wrote a novel about her

children.content: Debra Adelaide became friends with Gabrielle Carey when they were 12. After Carey died by suicide five decades later, Adelaide tried to address her unanswered questions in a novel.

image.altText: A woman with long grey hair and wearing a green long-sleeved top, sitting a garden and patting a dog.

items.title: Small oil refinery in outback Queensland could be on cusp of expansion

children.content: The Inland Oil Refinery produces specialised underground mining fuel, heating oils and other speciality solvents, and supplies a small amount of on-road diesel for the local community

gallery.altText: aerial of oil refinery and truck

thumbnail.altText: aerial of oil refinery and truck

items.title: Milk prices may need to increase to cover soaring fuel and fertiliser costs

children.content: Dairy farmers across Australia are battling crippling fuel and fertiliser costs. The industry is now calling for a 30-cent-per-litre rise in milk prices on supermarket shelves.

image.altText: A line of black and white cows lean their heads into a feeding trough. There is yellow hay in the trough.

items.title: Greyhound trainers frustrated as safety issues continue at Queensland track

children.content: Ipswich facility The Q was promised to be "as safe for dogs and trainers as possible", but several dogs died during its trial period and events have been cancelled due to unsafe conditions.

image.altText: a greyhound racing track at sunset

items.title: Locals fear church sale could bulldoze history

children.content: Residents on a suburban street in Western Sydney fear 130 years of history will be erased if a 19th century church is bulldozed to build new homes.

image.altText: Glenda Davis, Amanda and Clark Stone outside Jamisontown Church

items.title: How the long bomb is making an impact this AFL season

children.content: In a league where inside-50s are so often king, teams with prowess from outside the arc are making a major impact to start the 2026 AFL season.

image.altText: Willem Duursma kicks from outside 50m

items.title: Deep in a Ugandan forest, chimpanzees wage a lethal 'civil war'

children.content: A decades-long study reveals hundreds of chimpanzees waging a violent and deadly conflict against their former friends in their remote forest home.

image.altText: Looking through a frame of green leaves at one chimpanzee showing its teeth and another chimp watching on.

gallery.altText: a chimpanzee with its mouth open in the forest

thumbnail.altText: a chimpanzee with its mouth open in the forest

items.title: Kennedy becomes first Australian to break 10-second barrier at home

children.content: Lachlan Kennedy has scorched to a sub-10-second run in the heats of the 100 metres at the Australian Athletics Championships, and promises his best is still to come.

image.altText: Sprinter Lachlan Kennedy smiles and points at the camera with two outstretched hands

items.title: Fresh push for Murray Basin freight rail to get more produce off trucks

children.content: North-west Victorian farmers are pushing for the completion of the Murray Basin Rail Project, more than seven years after it was originally slated to be finished.

image.altText: old broad gauge rail track near Manangatang in Victoria's west

items.title: Country primary school staggered by 'routine' $100k water bills

children.content: Dunsborough Primary School is racking up massive water bills in comparison to nearby schools, to the detriment of student support and repairs to facilities.

image.altText: The entrance at Dunsborough Primary School

items.title: Fishhook injuries are common. How do you treat them?

children.content: What do you do when the hook catches you instead of the fish? Charter fishers and a medical expert weigh in.

image.altText: Rusty fishhook held over a tacklebox.

items.title: Bricks and brutalism being added to heritage list, despite objection of owners

children.content: More modern buildings are being added to South Australia's state heritage list, especially churches, but some owners object to their properties being preserved, worried that it will add cost and complexity to their plans.

gallery.altText: A concrete building with a metal roof

thumbnail.altText: A concrete building with a metal roof

items.title: St Kilda's Lance Collard found guilty of using homophobic slur against opponent

children.content: St Kilda forward Lance Collard has been found guilty of using a homophobic slur against an opponent, leaving his AFL career hanging in the balance.

image.altText: Lance Collard stands with both arms in the air during a St Kilda vs West Coast Eagles AFL match in 2025.

items.title: Commuters trapped for hours in freeway gridlock blast lack of signage

children.content: Commuters stuck queuing for up to four hours following a truck crash on the South Eastern Freeway on Thursday blast the transport department over a lack of signs warning them of the road closure ahead.

image.altText: Cars fill a freeway

items.title: Friends, family thank the community for helping to find missing woman

children.content: The family and friends of a woman who was missing for a week in south-western Victoria have thanked the community after she was found safe and well.

image.altText: collage of four photos of a woman with black hair

items.title: Snoozing seal blocks road in popular coastal spot

children.content: Locals say they are not surprised the seal, described as a regular visitor, decided to stop for a snooze on the roadway.

gallery.altText: A seal sleeping on a road.

thumbnail.altText: A seal sleeping on a road.
